questions.answer: Lotus Technology Inc. 是一家注册在开曼群岛的控股公司，主要通过其在中国和欧洲的子公司开展业务。截至最新的重组，Lotus Technology 的中国大陆业务由其在中国大陆的子公司运营，不再通过可变利益实体（VIE）结构进行。Lotus Technology Inc. 通过其全资子公司和整合的可变利益实体及其子公司的集团，主要从事设计、开发和销售豪华电池电动汽车（BEV 生活方式车型）以及豪华跑车的销售。此外，集团还提供其他销售和辅助服务。以下是 Lotus Technology Inc. 的主要子公司及其注册地：Lotus Advanced Technology Limited（注册地：香港）、Lotus Technology International Limited（注册地：香港）、Lotus Technology Innovative Limited（注册地：英国）、Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited（注册地：英国）、Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH（注册地：德国）、Lotus Cars Europe B.V.（注册地：荷兰）、Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd.（注册地：中国）、Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd.（注册地：中国）、Wuhan Lotus Cars Sales Limited（注册地：中国）、Wuhan Lotus Private Fund Management Co., Ltd.（注册地：中国）、Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd.（注册地：中国）、Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd.（注册地：中国）。这些子公司覆盖了多个业务领域，包括技术研发、车辆制造、销售和服务，体现了 Lotus Technology Inc. 的全球业务布局。以上信息基于2024年9月19日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: What is the company structure of Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the company structure of Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: Since early 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has been implementing a series of transactions to restructure its organization and business operations, which is referred to as the “Restructuring.” In connection with the Restructuring, the WFOE, the former VIE, and nominee equity holders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements, or the VIE Restructuring Agreements, pursuant to which the WFOE acquired 100% equity interest in the former VIE’s subsidiaries. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology Inc.'s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and it does not have any VIE structure. The holding company structure involves unique risks to investors. As a holding company, Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) may rely on dividends from its subsidiaries for cash requirements, including any payment of dividends to its shareholders. The ability of subsidiaries of LTC to pay dividends or make distributions to LTC may be restricted by laws and regulations applicable to them or the debt they incur on their own behalf or the instruments governing their debt. In addition, PRC regulatory authorities could disallow this holding company structure and limit or hinder LTC’s ability to conduct its business through, receive dividends or distributions from, or transfer funds to, the operating companies or list on a U.S. or other foreign exchange, which could result in a material adverse change in LTC’s operations and cause the value of the securities of LTC to significantly decline.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology issued 216,700,000 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark. The above reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022. On November 12, 2021, the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and a third party established Ningbo Robotics Co., Ltd., or Ningbo Robotics, in which the former VIE held a 60% equity interest. In March 2022, the former VIE transferred its 60% legal equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to its then wholly-owned subsidiary, Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. On March 15, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation declared a 10-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend, and such stock dividend was distributed to all the shareholders of Lotus Technology Corporation in proportion to their respective shareholdings in Lotus Technology Corporation. Before the stock dividend, Lotus Technology Corporation had 216,700,000 ordinary shares and 2,407,778 Series Pre-A Preferred Shares issued and outstanding with a par value of US$0.00001 per share. After the stock dividend, Lotus Technology Corporation had 2,167,000,000 ordinary shares and 24,077,780 Series Pre-A Preferred Shares issued and outstanding. Since early 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation has been implementing the Restructuring. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology Corporation’s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries, and Lotus Technology Corporation does not have any Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure. rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sales of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. The Group also provides other sales and ancillary services to customers.

rag_info.chunk_content:
[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Lotus Technology Corporate Structure Overview  
- **Table Summary**: This diagram provides a visual representation of the corporate structure of Lotus Technology Inc. as of the date of the annual report. It outlines the relationships and equity ownership between Lotus Technology Inc., its offshore subsidiaries, and its onshore subsidiaries in various regions including Hong Kong, the UK, Germany, the Netherlands, and mainland China.  
- **Context**: Before the diagram, the text discusses caution regarding forward-looking statements and mentions the dynamic risks inherent in Lotus Technology Inc.’s operations. After the diagram, it describes Lotus Technology Inc.’s Cayman Islands holding framework and its operational transitions, particularly changes following the 2023 Restructuring.  
- **Special Notes**: The diagram makes a clear distinction between “Offshore” and “Onshore” entities using a dashed line. All entities are shown with 100% equity interest relationships.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Lotus Technology Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands, is the parent holding company at the top of the corporate structure.  
- **Row 2**: Lotus Technology International Limited (Hong Kong) and Lotus Advanced Technology Limited (Hong Kong) are wholly owned subsidiaries of Lotus Technology Inc. These subsidiaries represent “Offshore” entities in the corporate hierarchy.  
- **Row 3**: Lotus Advanced Technology Limited holds 100% equity interest in Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., which is delineated as an “Onshore” subsidiary in mainland China.  
- **Row 4**: Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. has multiple wholly owned onshore subsidiaries: Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd., Wuhan Lotus Cars Sales Limited, Wuhan Lotus Private Fund Management Co., Ltd., Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd., and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd.  
- **Row 5**: In addition to its onshore connections, Lotus Advanced Technology Limited also holds 100% ownership in several offshore subsidiaries: Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited (UK), Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH (Germany), and Lotus Cars Europe B.V. (Netherlands).  
- **Row 6**: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (UK) is another offshore subsidiary directly owned by Lotus Technology Inc.  

This corporate structure shows clear ownership chains and demarcates offshore and onshore entities within Lotus Technology Inc.’s operational framework.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is not an operating company but a Cayman Islands holding company. Lotus Technology Inc. conducts its operations through its subsidiaries in mainland China and Europe, and for the periods ended prior to the 2023 Restructuring, also through the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and its subsidiaries based in mainland China. Prior to 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. relied on contractual arrangements among Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. (the WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and its nominee shareholders to direct the business operations of the former VIE and its subsidiaries. As a result, for the periods ended prior to the Restructuring, the former VIE’s financial results are consolidated in Lotus Technology Inc.'s consolidated and combined financial statements under U.S. GAAP for accounting purposes. Following the Restructuring, Lotus Technology Inc.'s operations in mainland China are currently conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries. In connection with the Restructuring, Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. (the WFOE), the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE), and nominee shareholders of the former VIE entered into a series of agreements, or the VIE Restructuring Agreements, in early 2023, pursuant to which, (i) Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd. acquired 100% equity interest in Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. and Hangzhou Lotus Technology Service Co., Ltd., each being a subsidiary of the former VIE;

rag_info.chunk_content: As of the date of this annual report, Lotus Technology Corporation’s operations in mainland China are conducted by its mainland China subsidiaries and Lotus Technology Corporation does not have any Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure. On February 22, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation consummated the Business Combination with LCAA, pursuant to the Merger Agreement. LCAA was a blank check company incorporated for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses or entities. Lotus Technology Corporation's ADSs and Warrants have been listed on The Nasdaq Global Market since February 23, 2024 under the symbol “LOT” and “LOTWW,” respectively. The mailing address of Lotus Technology Corporation's principal executive office is No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and its phone number is +8621 5466-6258. Lotus Technology Corporation's corporate website address is www.group-lotus.com. The information contained in, or accessible through, Lotus Technology Corporation's website does not constitute a part of this annual report. The SEC maintains an internet site that contains reports, proxy and information statements, and other information regarding issuers, such as Lotus Technology Corporation, that file electronically, with the SEC at www.sec.gov.

rag_info.chunk_content: Pursuant to the Mergers above stated, LCAA was considered as the “acquired” company for financial reporting purposes. Accordingly, for accounting purposes, the financial statements of LCAA represented a continuation of its operations with the Mergers treated as the equivalent of LCAA issuing shares for the net assets of LCAA, accompanied by a recapitalization. The net assets of LCAA are stated at historical cost, with no goodwill or other intangible assets recorded. The ordinary shares of LCAA and the Public Warrants are listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC, or “Nasdaq,” under the trading symbols “LOT” and “LOTWW,” respectively, on February 23, 2024. On November 11, 2021, Lotus Technology issued 95,990,886 ordinary shares to Lotus Technology International Investment Limited, ultimately 100% owned by Geely Holding. Through a series of reorganization steps (the “Reorganization”), including transferring the assets and employees in the Lotus BEV business unit of Ningbo Geely R&D into Wuhan Lotus Cars and transferring the equity of Wuhan Lotus Cars to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology gained control over the WFOE through Lotus HK on December 15, 2021. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus Technology GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021 and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the company structure of Lotus Technology?

rag_info.chunk_content: Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Technology received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology conducts the business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology issued 47,995,443 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark licenses. The above Reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022. The Reorganization consists of transferring the Lotus BEV business unit to the Group. Before and after the Reorganization, the Lotus BEV business unit was ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu. Accordingly, the Reorganization is accounted for under common control transaction. Therefore, the accompanying consolidated and combined financial statements include the assets, liabilities, revenue, expenses, and cash flows of the Lotus BEV business unit for the periods presented and are prepared as if the corporate structure of the Group after the Reorganization had been in existence throughout the periods presented. As mentioned in note 21, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology entered into a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited (“LCL”), a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”) and ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of Lotus Technology, pursuant to which, Lotus Technology was appointed as the exclusive global distributor (excluding in the United States of America) to distribute certain models of vehicles produced by LCL and to provide after-sale services and brand, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Holding Company Structure and China Operations 
summary: The corporate structure of Lotus Technology Inc., as outlined in its annual report, reveals a comprehensive hierarchy that includes a parent holding company based in the Cayman Islands and various subsidiaries across offshore and onshore locations. The diagram illustrates the 100% equity ownership relationships among these entities, which include subsidiaries in Hong Kong, the UK, Germany, the Netherlands, and mainland China.

At the top of the structure is Lotus Technology Inc., which wholly owns two Hong Kong subsidiaries: Lotus Technology International Limited and Lotus Advanced Technology Limited. The latter manages Wuhan Lotus Technology Co., Ltd., an onshore subsidiary that oversees several others, including Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd. Additionally, Lotus Advanced Technology Limited owns several offshore subsidiaries, such as Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited (UK), Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH (Germany), and Lotus Cars Europe B.V. (Netherlands). Another offshore entity, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, is directly owned by Lotus Technology Inc.

The report highlights significant operational changes following a 2023 restructuring, which eliminated the previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure. This restructuring allowed Lotus Technology Inc. to directly manage its operations in mainland China through its subsidiaries, enhancing its operational efficiency. The restructuring involved the WFOE acquiring full equity interests in former VIE subsidiaries and transferring associated assets and liabilities, thereby terminating the VIE Agreements.

Despite these structural improvements, the company faces considerable risks in conducting business in mainland China, including regulatory approvals for offshore offerings, antimonopoly actions, and cybersecurity concerns. These factors could adversely impact operations, limit foreign investments, and affect the company's ability to list on U.S. stock exchanges. The evolving legal landscape in China adds further uncertainty, with potential implications for business performance and the value of securities. Overall, while the restructuring aims to streamline operations, the company remains vigilant about the regulatory challenges inherent in the Chinese market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. History and Development of the Company 
summary: The Lotus BEV business, established in 2018, operates through various entities, including Wuhan Lotus Cars and Ningbo Geely R&D in China, as well as Lotus Tech UK and Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH in Germany. In 2023, the company underwent a significant restructuring, eliminating its previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure, which had been used for operations in mainland China. This restructuring included the establishment of LTC as a limited liability company in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021, and the transfer of assets and employees to create a Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) from Wuhan Lotus Cars. The company acquired equity interests in Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH in late 2021 and mid-2022, respectively.

Additionally, a trademark license agreement with Group Lotus Limited was finalized in June 2022, allowing LTC to use the "Lotus" trademark for lifestyle vehicles, which included the issuance of 216,700,000 ordinary shares as compensation. In March 2022, LTC executed a 10-for-1 stock split, increasing its ordinary shares to 2,167,000,000. 

As of early 2023, LTC's operations in mainland China are now managed through its subsidiaries without the VIE structure. The company completed a Business Combination with LCAA on February 22, 2024, enabling its American Depositary Shares and Warrants to begin trading on The Nasdaq Global Market the following day. The principal executive office is located in Shanghai, China, and further information can be accessed through the SEC's electronic filing system.

Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The mailing address of Lotus Technology’s principal executive office is No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, People’s Republic of China, and its phone number is +86 21 5466-6258. Lotus Technology’s corporate website address is www.group-lotus.com. metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Corporate History and Structure 
summary: The Lotus BEV business, founded in 2018, operates through various entities including Wuhan Lotus Cars, Ningbo Geely R&D in China, Lotus Tech UK, and Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH in Germany. Following a significant restructuring in early 2023, the company has eliminated its previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure, which had been used to manage operations in mainland China. The restructuring involved the establishment of LTC as a limited liability company in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021, and a series of asset and employee transfers that culminated in full control over its wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) by December 2021. 

In November 2021, LTC entered into a trademark license agreement with Group Lotus Limited, allowing it to use the "Lotus" trademark for lifestyle vehicles, excluding sports cars, in exchange for issuing 216,700,000 ordinary shares. A stock split on March 15, 2022, increased the number of ordinary shares to 2,167,000,000. The restructuring process in 2023 further streamlined operations, ensuring that all activities in mainland China are now managed solely through its subsidiaries, without reliance on the VIE structure. Consequently, LTC's financial results are now consolidated solely from its subsidiaries, with the company asserting that these changes will not materially impact its operations or financial performance.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

title: About Lotus
Technology 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a luxury lifestyle electric vehicle manufacturer with operations in the UK, EU, and China. The company focuses on cutting-edge research and development in advanced automotive technologies, particularly in electrification and digitalization. For more information, you can visit their website at www.group-lotus.com.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sales of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. The Group also provides other sales and ancillary services to customers.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: or (b) the sale, lease, exclusive license or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of the Group or the licensing of all or substantially all of the Group’s intellectual property, the holders of redeemable convertible preferred shares shall be entitled to receive, prior and in preference to any distribution or payment that shall be made to the holders of any ordinary shares, the liquidation preference amount per share is equal to (i) 100% of the issuance price, plus (ii) interest at a simple rate of eight percent (8%) per annum accrued on the issuance price during the period from the issuance date to the liquidation payment date, however, that the portion relevant to the Pre-A Investor A’s initial investment amount of RMB 150,000 shall accrue the interest from November 29, 2021, plus (iii) any declared but unpaid dividends, plus (iv) interest at a simple rate of three percent (3%) per annum accrued on the WFOE Exchangeable Notes during the period from November 5, 2021 to the issuance date, only applicable to the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder. If Lotus Technology’s available funds and assets are insufficient for the full payment of liquidation preference amounts to all holders of redeemable convertible preferred shares, then Lotus Technology’s entire available funds and assets shall be distributed ratably among the holders of redeemable convertible preferred shares in proportion to the aggregate liquidation preference amounts such holders are otherwise entitled to receive. Liquidation preference is as follows in sequence: Series A Preferred Shares, Series Pre-A Preferred Shares.

DESCRIPTION OF BUSINESS AND ORGANIZATION (viii) 8,427,821 ordinary shares were issued to then holders of Class A ordinary shares of LCAA, including 1,265,103 Class A ordinary shares of LCAA held by public shareholders and 7,087,718 Class A ordinary shares of LCAA held by LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP (the “Sponsor”) and 75,000 Class A ordinary shares of LCAA held by the directors and officers of LCAA, among which all or a portion of (as reasonably determined by LCAA in good faith) 20% of the ordinary shares held by the Sponsor will remain unvested upon the consummation of the Merger Transaction and become vested upon each occurrence of (a) the commencement or official announcement of any business collaboration facilitated by the Sponsor or its affiliates (including, without limitation, in connection with product development, marketing, customer engagement, retail space, and technology infrastructure development), or (b) an approved commitment to invest in LCAA or one of its subsidiaries by an investor introduced or facilitated by the Sponsor or its affiliate, in each case, within eighteen months following the Closing Date; 9,550,246 warrants were issued to the then public shareholders of LCAA in exchange for the warrants held by public shareholders of LCAA (the “Public Warrants”), and 5,486,784 warrants were issued to LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP in exchange for warrants held by the Sponsor (the “Sponsor Warrants”). Each Public Warrant and Sponsor Warrant can be exercised at an exercise price of US$11.50 in exchange for one ordinary share of LCAA.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the Mergers above stated, LCAA was considered as the “acquired” company for financial reporting purposes. Accordingly, for accounting purposes, the financial statements of LCAA represented a continuation of its operations with the Mergers treated as the equivalent of LCAA issuing shares for the net assets of LCAA, accompanied by a recapitalization. The net assets of LCAA are stated at historical cost, with no goodwill or other intangible assets recorded. The ordinary shares of LCAA and the Public Warrants are listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC, or “Nasdaq,” under the trading symbols “LOT” and “LOTWW,” respectively, on February 23, 2024. On November 11, 2021, Lotus Technology issued 95,990,886 ordinary shares to Lotus Technology International Investment Limited, ultimately 100% owned by Geely Holding. Through a series of reorganization steps (the “Reorganization”), including transferring the assets and employees in the Lotus BEV business unit of Ningbo Geely R&D into Wuhan Lotus Cars and transferring the equity of Wuhan Lotus Cars to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology gained control over the WFOE through Lotus HK on December 15, 2021. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus Technology GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021 and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu.

On August 9, 2021, Lotus Technology's wholly owned subsidiary, WFOE, the VIE, and the VIE’s nominee equity holders entered into a contractual agreement, which was replaced by a series of contractual arrangements entered into by and among the WFOE, the VIE, and the nominee equity holders on March 8, 2022, including (i) Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement, (ii) Exclusive Purchase Option Agreement, (iii) Equity Pledge Agreement, (iv) Powers of Attorney, and (v) Spousal Consent Letters. The above agreements are collectively referred to as Variable Interest Entity (VIE) Arrangements. Through the VIE Arrangements, the nominee equity holders of the VIE granted all their legal rights, including voting rights and disposition rights of their equity interests in the VIE, to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE). The nominee equity holders of the VIE did not participate in income and loss and did not have the power to direct the activities of the VIE that most significantly impact the VIE’s economic performance. Accordingly, the VIE was considered a variable interest entity. Because the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) has (i) the power to direct activities of the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) that most significantly impact the economic performance of the VIE;

Under the terms of the VIE Arrangements, Lotus Technology, through the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), has (i) the right to receive economic benefits that could potentially be significant to the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) in the form of service fees under the Exclusive Consulting and Service Agreement; (ii) the right to unconditionally receive all dividends or interest declared by the VIE and all of the assets of the VIE; (iii) the right to receive the benefits of the VIE through its exclusive option to acquire 100% of the equity interests in the VIE, to the extent permitted under respective laws and regulations. Accordingly, the financial statements of the VIE are included in Lotus Technology’s consolidated and combined financial statements. Under the terms of the VIE Arrangements, the Variable Interest Entity’s nominee equity holders have no rights to the net assets nor have the obligations to fund the deficit, and such rights and obligations have been vested to Lotus Technology through the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE). All of the deficit (net liabilities) and net loss of the Variable Interest Entities are attributed to Lotus Technology through the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE). The principal terms of the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) Arrangements are as follows: Lotus Technology relies on the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) Arrangements to operate and control VIEs.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: All of the VIE Arrangements are governed by PRC laws and provide for the resolution of disputes through arbitration under PRC laws. Accordingly, these agreements would be interpreted in accordance with PRC laws and any disputes would be resolved in accordance with PRC legal procedures. Uncertainties in the PRC legal system could limit Lotus Technology’s ability to enforce these VIE Arrangements. In the event that Lotus Technology is unable to enforce these VIE Arrangements, or if Lotus Technology suffers significant time delays or other obstacles in the process of enforcing these VIE Arrangements, it would be difficult to exert effective control over VIEs, and Lotus Technology’s ability to conduct its business and the results of operations and financial condition may be materially and adversely affected. In the opinion of management, based on the legal opinion obtained from Lotus Technology’s PRC legal counsel, the above Variable Interest Entity (VIE) Arrangements were legally binding and enforceable and did not violate current PRC laws and regulations. However, there are substantial uncertainties regarding the interpretation and application of current and future PRC laws and regulations.

The footnote (i) explains that net loss includes other expenses, particularly those arising from VIE asset transfers to Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) subsidiaries, eliminated within consolidation.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In June 2016, the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) issued ASU No. 2016-13, Financial Instruments—Credit Losses (Topic 326): Measurement of Credit Losses on Financial Instruments (“ASC 326”), which amends previously issued guidance regarding the impairment of financial instruments by creating an impairment model that is based on expected losses rather than incurred losses. The new accounting standard does not apply to loans and receivables between entities under common control. Lotus Technology Group adopted ASC 326 and several associated ASUs on January 1, 2023, using a modified retrospective approach with the cumulative effect recorded as an increase of accumulated deficit in the amount of US$15. Lotus Technology Group assessed that accounts receivable, other receivables, loan receivables, short-term and long-term deposits are within the scope of ASC 326. Lotus Technology Group has identified the relevant risk characteristics of its customers and suppliers, and the related accounts receivable, loan receivables, other current assets, and other non-current assets, which include size, type of the services or the products Lotus Technology Group provides, or a combination of these characteristics. Receivables with similar risk characteristics have been grouped into pools. For each pool, Lotus Technology Group considers the historical credit loss experience, current economic conditions, supportable forecasts of future economic conditions, and any recoveries in assessing the lifetime expected credit losses, etc. Other key factors that influence the expected credit loss analysis include customer demographics, payment terms offered in the normal course of business to customers, and industry-specific factors that could impact Lotus Technology Group’s receivables.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Additionally, external data and macroeconomic factors are also considered. This is assessed at each quarter based on Lotus Technology Group’s specific facts and circumstances. Lotus Technology Group considers historical credit loss rates for each category of deposits and other receivables and also considers forward-looking macroeconomic data in making its loss accrual determinations. Accounting guidance also describes three main approaches to measuring the fair value of assets and liabilities: (1) market approach; (2) income approach; and (3) cost approach. The market approach uses prices and other relevant information generated from market transactions involving identical or comparable assets or liabilities. The income approach uses valuation techniques to convert future amounts to a single present value amount. The measurement is based on the value indicated by current market expectations about those future amounts. The cost approach is based on the amount that would currently be required to replace an asset. Practical expedients and exemptions Lotus Technology Group follows the guidance on immaterial promises when identifying performance obligations in vehicle sales and concludes that the free pick-up and delivery service for maintenance, door-to-door troubleshooting, and lifetime roadside assistance are immaterial and therefore Lotus Technology Group does not assess whether these promised services are performance obligations. When reaching this conclusion, Lotus Technology Group considered that these promises are value-added services to enhance customer experience rather than critical items for vehicle driving and forecasted that usage of these services will be very limited.

title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Group issued put options to each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika at nil consideration. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology Group to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of LGIL for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of LGIL as of December 31, 2024 (the “exercise price”), if the total number of vehicles sold by LGIL in 2024 exceeds 5,000 (the “exercise condition”). Geely HK and Etika are entitled to exercise the put option on an individual standalone basis. If Geely HK or Etika exercises the put option, Geely HK or Etika shall put all its equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd to Lotus Technology Group. The exercise price will be settled with Lotus Technology Group’s newly issued ordinary shares. The number of ordinary shares of Lotus Technology Group to be issued to each of Geely HK and Etika shall be equal to the quotient of (a) the exercise price, divided by (b) the per share listing price, i.e. US$10 per share, of ordinary shares of Lotus Technology Group.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The put options are determined to be freestanding financial instruments that are accounted for as liabilities in the scope of ASC Topic 480, Distinguishing Liabilities from Equity, because the put options embody a conditional obligation that Lotus Technology Group may settle by issuing a variable number of its equity shares and the monetary value of the obligation is based on variation in LGIL’s financial performance other than fair value of Lotus Technology Group’s equity shares. Lotus Technology Group initially measured the put option liabilities at fair value and subsequently remeasured to fair value each period with changes recognized in profit or loss. At initial recognition, Lotus Technology Group recognized the issuance of put options at its fair value of US$9,376 as dividends to the shareholders with a corresponding decrease in additional paid-in capital. The increase in fair value of the put option liabilities in the amount of US$2,508, nil and nil for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022 and 2021 was recognized in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss, respectively. all_retrieved_content.page_content: PIPE Investments In 2023, Lotus Technology Group entered into subscription agreements (the “PIPE Subscription Agreements”) with Lotus Technology International Investment Limited (“LTIIL”), ultimately controlled by Geely Holding, Etika, and other investors, pursuant to which LTIIL, Etika, and other investors have committed to subscribe for and purchase Lotus Technology Group’s ordinary shares at \$10.00 per share for an aggregate investment amount of US\$127,500, US\$122,500, and US\$94,713, respectively (“PIPE Investments”). The obligations of the parties to consummate the PIPE Investments are subject to the satisfaction or waiver of certain customary closing conditions of the respective parties, including, among others, (i) all conditions precedent under the Merger Agreement having been satisfied or waived (other than those to be satisfied at the closing of the Business Combination), (ii) the accuracy of representations and warranties in all material respects, (iii) material compliance with covenants; and (iv) all of the consents from, and filings and/or registrations with, applicable governmental authority as required by applicable PRC laws in connection with the PIPE Investors’ outbound direct investment in Lotus Technology Group shall have been duly obtained and completed. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Group received US\$10,000 refundable deposits in connection with the PIPE Investments, bearing a simple interest rate of 8% per annum. As of December 31, 2023, no shares have been issued pursuant to the Meritz Subscription Agreement and the PIPE Subscription Agreements.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title:** Summary of Exchangeable Notes Balances and Movements (2021-2023)  
- **Table Summary:** The table provides a breakdown of the balances and movements of exchangeable notes issued by Lotus Technology Group's subsidiary, WFOE, and the Lightning Speed entity. It details issuance amounts, changes in fair values, forex adjustments, and conversions. The data spans from January 1, 2021, to December 31, 2023, and includes both current and non-current portions as of the most recent balance.  
- **Context:** Leading up to the table, the text describes Lotus Technology Group's financing arrangements, including refundable PIPE deposits and the issuance of exchangeable notes by WFOE with various tranches, maturities, and amounts. The repayment guarantees and a notable conversion of RMB 400,000 worth of notes to Series A redeemable convertible preferred shares are also highlighted.  
- **Special Notes:** Amounts are expressed in US dollars (US$). Notes include details on changes due to instrument-specific credit risks, fair value fluctuations, and foreign currency translation adjustments. Footnotes reference specific accounting treatments (e.g., Note 17 for redeemable convertible preferred shares).  

---

[Row Level]  

**Balance as of January 1, 2021:**  
Row 1: The opening balance for exchangeable notes in 2021 was zero for both WFOE and Lightning Speed entities, resulting in a total balance of US$0.  

**Issuance of Exchangeable Notes (2021):**  
Row 2: During 2021, WFOE issued exchangeable notes amounting to US$125,039, while no notes were issued by Lightning Speed, leading to a total issuance of US$125,039.  

**Changes in Fair Values (Excluding Credit Risk) (2021):**  
Row 3: Adjustments to fair values of WFOE exchangeable notes, excluding credit risk, increased the balance by US$1,065. Lightning Speed had no related adjustments, resulting in a total increase of US$1,065.  

**Changes in Credit Risk (2021):**  
Row 4: Fair value changes due to instrument-specific credit risk caused a reduction of US$132 for WFOE exchangeable notes, with no such adjustments for Lightning Speed, yielding a total decrease of US$132.  

**Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment (2021):**  
Row 5: A translation gain of US$448 was recognized for WFOE exchangeable notes due to foreign currency movements, with no adjustments for Lightning Speed, adding US$448 to the total.  

**Balance as of December 31, 2021:**  
Row 6: As of year-end 2021, the total balance of exchangeable notes stood at US$126,420, consisting entirely of WFOE notes (US$126,420 for WFOE and US$0 for Lightning Speed).  

---

**Issuance of Exchangeable Notes (2022):**  
Row 7: In 2022, WFOE issued US$307,172 worth of notes, and Lightning Speed issued US$71,792, resulting in a total issuance of US$378,964.  

**Exchange to Series A Shares (Note 17) (2022):**  
Row 8: A portion of WFOE's issued notes, valued at US$60,419, was converted into Series A redeemable convertible preferred shares during 2022.  

**Changes in Fair Values (Excluding Credit Risk) (2022):**  
Row 9: WFOE experienced a gain in fair values (US$13,162), while Lightning Speed remained unaffected, resulting in a total fair value increase of US$13,162.  

**Changes in Credit Risk (2022):**  
Row 10: Changes related to instrument-specific credit risk resulted in a US$33 increase in WFOE's notes, with no impact on Lightning Speed, creating a total gain of US$33.  

**Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment (2022):**  
Row 11: A foreign currency translation loss of US$31,048 was recorded for WFOE notes, while Lightning Speed did not register any such adjustments.  

**Balance as of December 31, 2022:**  
Row 12: By the end of 2022, the combined balance for exchangeable notes was US$427,112, comprising US$355,320 from WFOE and US$71,792 from Lightning Speed.  

---

**Issuance of Exchangeable Notes (2023):**  
Row 13: In 2023, WFOE issued US$737 in additional notes, while Lightning Speed issued US$3,711, leading to a total issuance of US$4,448.  

**Changes in Fair Values (Excluding Credit Risk) (2023):**  
Row 14: Adjustments to fair values, excluding credit risk, increased WFOE notes by US$272 and Lightning Speed notes by US$1,398, resulting in a total increase of US$1,670.  

**Changes in Credit Risk (2023):**  
Row 15: WFOE notes saw a reduction of US$5,574 due to instrument-specific credit risk, while Lightning Speed notes experienced a reduction of US$1,223, totaling a combined drop of US$6,797.  

**Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment (2023):**  
Row 16: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in no recorded gain or loss in 2023 for either WFOE or Lightning Speed.  

**Balance as of December 31, 2023:**  
Row 17: The final balance for 2023 amounted to US$454,316, with US$378,638 from WFOE and US$75,678 from Lightning Speed.  

**Current vs. Non-Current (2023):**  
Row 18: The current portion of exchangeable notes was US$378,638, while the non-current portion accounted for US$75,678, based on the balance as of December 31, 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In September 2021, Lotus Technology Group’s subsidiary, WFOE, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, WFOE is entitled to issue, from time to time, exchangeable notes (the “WFOE Exchangeable Notes”) to obtain financing from the investor (the “WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder”) up to RMB3,000,000 with a coupon rate of 3% per annum. Each tranche of WFOE Exchangeable Notes is scheduled to mature on the one-year anniversary date of issuance. The repayments of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by Founders Onshore Vehicle. Lotus Technology Group issued RMB800,000 (equivalent to US$125,039), RMB600,000 (equivalent to US$94,130), RMB600,000 (equivalent to US$94,457), RMB800,000 (equivalent to US$118,585), and RMB200,000 (equivalent to US$27,883) on November 5, 2021, January 7, 2022, January 18, 2022, August 2, 2022, and September 22, 2023, to the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder, respectively. Among these, RMB400,000 issued in November 2021 was exchanged for Series A redeemable convertible preferred shares in December 2022, as mentioned in note 17. At the time when there is a subsequent round of equity financing, upon the notification in writing by Lotus Technology Group, the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder is entitled to convert the whole or any portion of the outstanding principal amount of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes into the shares of the subsequent round of equity financing at the post-money equity valuation based on a fixed monetary amount.

The fair value of the guarantees and Rights and Obligations provided by the Founders Onshore Vehicle was US$909 for the tranche issued in September 2023, US$8,135 for the tranches issued during the year ended December 31, 2022, and US$3,391 for the tranche issued in November 2021. The fair value of the guarantees and Rights and Obligations was treated as debt issuance cost and charged to the interest expenses in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss. On April 27, 2023, Lotus Technology Group entered into a subscription agreement with the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder (the “Jingkai Subscription Agreement”), pursuant to which the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder has committed to subscribe for and purchase Lotus Technology Group’s ordinary shares for US\$10.00 per share, for an investment amount of RMB2,600,000 (equivalent to US\$367,092), which will be settled by the then outstanding principal amount of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Key Assumptions for Valuation of WFOE Exchangeable Notes  
- **Table Summary**: This table presents the key financial and probabilistic assumptions used to estimate the fair value of WFOE Exchangeable Notes as of December 31, 2023, compared to December 31, 2022. The assumptions include risk-free interest rates, discount rates, probabilities related to conversion and qualified IPOs, and bond yield ranges.  
- **Context**: The valuation methodology leverages a scenario-weighted average approach, incorporating varying outcomes for settlement (cash or shares) and timing. Each scenario's valuation was derived from cash payment or equity equivalents, discounted appropriately.  
- **Special Notes**: All values are expressed in percentages, with bond yields presented as ranges. The "Probability of Conversion" for 2022 is presented in a range of three different values.

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: The risk-free interest rate as of December 31, 2023, is 1.75%, compared to 2.07% as of December 31, 2022.  
- **Row 2**: The discount rate remains consistent at 20.00% for both December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2022.  
- **Row 3**: The probability of conversion increased significantly in 2023, reaching 90.00%, compared to a range of 50.00%–75.00% in 2022.  
- **Row 4**: Bond yields for 2023 are in the range of 3.27%–4.06%, which is substantially lower than the 7.35%–7.76% range recorded for 2022.  
- **Row 5**: The probability of occurrence of a Qualified IPO climbed from 45.00% in 2022 to 90.00% in 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes In November 2022, Lotus Technology Group’s subsidiary, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. (“Lightning Speed”), entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Lightning Speed is entitled to issue exchangeable notes (the “Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes”) of RMB1,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor (the “Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder”). The repayments of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the immediate shareholders of Lightning Speed, i.e., Sanya Lotus Venture Investment Limited Company (“Sanya Lotus”), a wholly owned subsidiary of the VIE, and Ningbo Weili Enterprise Management Consulting Partnership (Limited Partnership). The WFOE provides a joint and several liability guarantee for the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes. In December 2022, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. issued the first tranche of RMB500,000 (equivalent to US$71,792) to the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder. Each tranche of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes is scheduled to mature on the five-year anniversary date of issuance and bears a simple interest rate of the loan prime rate published by the China Foreign Exchange Trade System (“LPR”). With the consent of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. can extend the term of each tranche of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes twice, but each extension shall not exceed one year. During the extension period, the interest on the outstanding principal of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes is calculated at the simple interest rate of 110% of LPR. All interest shall be paid at maturity.

summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The changes in fair values of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes due to the instrument-specific credit risk of US$1,398, nil and nil were debited to other comprehensive income (loss) and all other changes in fair values of US$3,711, nil and nil were recognized as “Changes in fair values of mandatorily redeemable noncontrolling interest, exchangeable notes and convertible notes, excluding impact of instrument-specific credit risk” in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022 and 2021, respectively. The Group adopted a scenario-weighted average method to estimate the fair value of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes, based on an analysis of future values of the settlement of the obligation, assuming various outcomes. The probability weightings assigned to certain potential scenarios were based on management’s assessment of the probability of settlement of the liability in cash or shares and an assessment of the timing of settlement. In each scenario, the obligation valuation was based on the contractually agreed cash payment or equivalent equity discounted to each valuation date. The fair values of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes are estimated with the following key assumptions used:

- **Table Summary**: The table outlines the detailed movements in the financial balances of different convertible notes, including the Series Pre-A Note, Subsidiary Convertible Note, and 2023 Convertible Notes, from 2021 through 2023. The data includes issuances, conversions, redemptions, interest payments, changes in fair values, and foreign currency translation adjustments, with final balances as of December 31 for each year.

[Row Level]

**2021 Activities:**
- **Row 1**: "At the beginning of 2021, the balance for the Series Pre-A Note, Subsidiary Convertible Note, and 2023 Convertible Notes was US$0 across all categories, indicating no convertible notes were outstanding."
- **Row 2**: "In 2021, the Group issued Series Pre-A Notes with a value of US$23,445, raising the total balance of convertible notes to US$23,445 by the end of the year."
- **Row 3**: "As of December 31, 2021, the total balance of convertible notes was US$23,445 (entirely attributed to Series Pre-A Notes), with no issuances or activity in the Subsidiary Convertible Note and 2023 Convertible Notes categories."

**2022 Activities:**
- **Row 4**: "During 2022, no new Series Pre-A Notes were issued. However, Subsidiary Convertible Notes worth US$75,037 were issued, contributing to the movement within that category."
- **Row 5**: "An amount of US$(23,445) was converted from the Series Pre-A Notes into Series Pre-A redeemable convertible preferred shares, nullifying the Series Pre-A Note balance by the end of 2022."
- **Row 6**: "Changes in fair values of the Subsidiary Convertible Notes, excluding instrument-specific credit risk, amounted to US$4,264, increasing the total balance."
- **Row 7**: "Changes in fair values due to the instrument-specific credit risk for Subsidiary Convertible Notes added a further US$890 to the balance."
- **Row 8**: "The Subsidiary Convertible Notes balance was reduced by US$(3,421) due to foreign currency translation adjustments."
- **Row 9**: "As of December 31, 2022, the cumulative balance of convertible notes was US$76,770, solely accounted for by Subsidiary Convertible Notes, as there was no new activity for the Series Pre-A Note or 2023 Convertible Notes."

**2023 Activities:**
- **Row 10**: "In 2023, US$25,297 worth of 2023 Convertible Notes were issued, contributing a significant balance to this new category."
- **Row 11**: "A partial redemption for 2023 Convertible Notes accounted for US$(5,648), reducing the balance in this category."
- **Row 12**: "Interest payments totaling US$(3,072) were made for 2023 Convertible Notes, further affecting the balance."
- **Row 13**: "Fair value adjustments, excluding instrument-specific credit risk, led to increases of US$2,376 (Subsidiary Convertible Notes) and US$616 (2023 Convertible Notes), adding a total of US$2,992."
- **Row 14**: "Changes in fair values due to specific credit risks increased the balance by US$12 for 2023 Convertible Notes and US$6,951 for Subsidiary Convertible Notes, netting US$6,963."
- **Row 15**: "Foreign currency translation adjustments decreased the total balance by US$(1,390) solely due to Subsidiary Convertible Notes."
- **Row 16**: "As of December 31, 2023, the total balance of convertible notes stood at US$101,912, comprising US$20,277 from 2023 Convertible Notes and US$81,635 from Subsidiary Convertible Notes. No balance remained for Series Pre-A Notes."
- **Row 17**: "Of the total balance as of December 31, 2023, US$20,277 represented the current portion, while US$81,635 was classified as the non-current portion."

all_retrieved_content.page_content: ● If there is any occurrence of events of default, the Subsidiary Convertible Note Holder has the right to require the immediate parent company of Ningbo Robotics and the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) to redeem all outstanding principal amounts of the Subsidiary Convertible Note and settle the unpaid interest immediately. The redemption price upon the occurrence of events of default equals (i) the outstanding principal, plus (ii) the outstanding principal × interest rate ÷ 365 × the number of days from the issuance date to the redemption date, less (iii) the interest already paid for the outstanding principal. If the immediate parent company of Ningbo Robotics or the VIE delays in payments, a 0.05% penalty per day of the overdue redemption price shall be paid to the Subsidiary Convertible Note Holder. During the years ended December 31, 2023, and 2022, no default events occurred. Lotus Technology and the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) provide a joint and several liability guarantee for the Subsidiary Convertible Note. Lotus Technology elected the fair value option for the Subsidiary Convertible Note, including the component related to accrued interest. Lotus Technology believes the fair value option best reflects the economics of the underlying transaction. The Subsidiary Convertible Note was recognized at fair value at the issuance date and is measured subsequently at fair value.
